data.title: BTCC eyes AI and RWA expansion in year 15 after $3.7 trillion volume record
data.description:
BTCC, a claimant to the title of the world’s longest-serving cryptocurrency exchange, reported record performance in 2025 with $3. 7 trillion in total trading volume and a global user base that reached 11 million, a 60% increase year-over-year.   As the exchange approaches its 15th anniversary in 2026, it is now signaling a shift in its focus […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/btcc-eyes-ai-and-rwa-expansion-in-year-15-after-3-7-trillion-volume-record/

data.title: South Korea's FSC Denies Proposal of 3% Capital Disclosure Rule for Digital Assets
data.description: South Korea's Financial Services Commission (FSC) has refuted claims that it proposed a 3% capital disclosure rule for corporate digital asset investments. According to NS3.AI, the FSC clarified that no decisions have been finalized concerning investment limits or disclosure standards. Ongoing discussions are taking place within a public-private task force about the involvement of professional investment firms in the virtual asset market.

data.title: Castle Island Ventures Partner: X Sacrificing Crypto Community Reach, a Digital Property Rights Dispute is Coming
data.description: Nic Carter, a partner at Castle Island Ventures, wrote on the X platform that "X is being dominated by a 'For You' algorithm. The platform is making trade-offs between increasing user dwell time and maintaining a stable connection between creators and fans, currently prioritizing the former. This is impacting creators' ability to continuously reach their existing audiences and reducing the predictability of users consistently receiving content from the creators they follow, thus impacting niche vertical communities (filter bubbles) like Crypto Twitter." Nic Carter predicts that future discussions and demands surrounding "digital property rights" may emerge, focusing on algorithmic transparency, creators' right to reach their followers, and users' right to receive content from the accounts they follow.

data.title: White House Faces Criticism Over Altered Protest Image
data.description: The White House has come under scrutiny for sharing an allegedly altered image of Nekima Levy Armstrong's arrest during a protest related to Immigration and Customs Enforcement. According to NS3.AI, Armstrong, who is associated with organizing a protest at a church in Minnesota, is facing federal charges under 18 USC 241. Despite public backlash over the manipulated imagery, the White House has defended its position on law enforcement and stated that 'memes will continue.' This stance persists amid ongoing discussions about regulating AI-generated political media.

data.title: Bybit will gradually cease providing services to Japanese residents.
data.description: Bybit announced a phased shutdown of its service for Japanese residents: starting March 23 at 11:00 AM, accounts will be switched to "liquidation-only" mode, and all open positions will be forcibly liquidated starting July 22 at 11:00 AM. (CoinPost)

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 90,000 USDT with a 0.12% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Jan 23, 2026, 04:14 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 90,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 90,000.179688 USDT, with a narrowed 0.12% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Pantera Capital Predicts Challenging 2025 for Cryptocurrencies with Recovery in 2026
data.description: Pantera Capital has released its outlook for 2026, forecasting a difficult year ahead for cryptocurrencies in 2025, particularly for non-Bitcoin tokens. According to NS3.AI, these tokens have faced declines due to weak value capture, decreased on-chain activity, and waning retail interest. The fund characterizes 2025 as a 'whipsaw' year, marked by market capitulation. Despite these challenges, Pantera Capital expects a recovery in the crypto markets to begin in 2026.

data.title: TAO Faces Critical Price Movement Amid Market Challenges
data.description: CyrilXBT posted on X. The cryptocurrency TAO has experienced a clear distribution at its peak, followed by a pattern of sustained lower highs and lower lows. The price has reverted to previous support levels, which have now become resistance. There is an urgent need for a price rebound to prevent further negative developments in the market.

data.title: Global Fund Managers' Cash Allocation Hits Record Low
data.description:
Phyrex posted on X. Global fund managers have reduced their cash allocation to a historic low of 3.2%, down from 3.3% a month ago. This indicates that fund managers are holding minimal cash reserves, suggesting a high level of investment and risk appetite. With low cash reserves, market pricing increasingly relies on favorable conditions. If the market direction remains unchanged, low cash levels can act as a stabilizing force, as investors are likely to buy on dips. However, if market conditions shift, the lack of cash can become a vulnerability, leading to asset sales and triggering a chain reaction of redemptions, stop-losses, and risk budget contractions.

This situation does not necessarily signal an immediate market peak. In December of last year, cash allocations were similarly low, yet U.S. stocks reached new highs a month later. However, these highs were not driven by institutional buying but rather by external funds or leveraged risk-taking. In the event of uncontrolled negative developments, the impact could be severe due to the lack of cash to absorb losses. The market dynamics are increasingly resembling a risk premium game, where positive developments require additional capital to drive growth, while negative news could be amplified due to crowded positions.

The best-case scenario involves a surge in FOMO (fear of missing out) sentiment, with retail investors increasing their purchasing power. Fund managers could then offload some assets to retail investors, allowing them to accumulate cash and re-enter the market when clear positive signals emerge. Conversely, the worst-case scenario mirrors the situation in April, where fund managers liquidated significant assets to rebuild cash reserves, awaiting better institutional opportunities.

data.title: High-Frequency Trader Closes Bitcoin Short Position with Loss
data.description:
On January 23, a high-frequency trader known as 'Pioneer of Short Selling' (0x3bcae) closed a short position of 68.95 BTC, incurring a loss of $14,000. According to BlockBeats, the trader currently holds only short positions with an unrealized loss of $15,000.

The address employs a high-frequency, multi-asset strategy, favoring overnight positions with strict risk management.

data.title: Bitcoin Network Sees Hashrate Increase Amid Rising Prices
data.description: The Bitcoin network's hashrate experienced a 3.4% increase this week, reaching an average of 1039 EH/s. According to NS3.AI, the average Bitcoin price also saw a slight rise of 0.61%, bringing it to approximately $92,872. In a notable development, Ethiopia is considering partnerships to establish Bitcoin mining operations, signaling a significant move in the industry. Furthermore, two independent miners successfully mined a block each, earning rewards of around $300,000.

data.title: Farcaster Protocol to Continue Operations Amid Acquisition
data.description: Farcaster co-founder Dan Romero has confirmed that the Farcaster protocol will continue to operate and will not be shut down. According to NS3.AI, the protocol's acquirer, Merkle, plans to return the $180 million previously raised from investors. Romero also addressed rumors regarding his recent home purchase, providing clarification on the matter.

data.title: This week's top mining news highlights: Ethiopia explores Bitcoin mining strategies; Two independent miners each produce a block and receive approximately $300,000 in rewards.
data.description: Week 4 of 2026 (January 16 - January 22): 1. According to cloverpool, the average hashrate of the Bitcoin network was 1039 EH/s, with a high of 1164 EH/s and a low of 947 EH/s, a 3.4% increase from the previous week's average hashrate (1005 EH/s). 2. According to blockchain.com, the average price of Bitcoin was $92,872, with a high of $95,830 and a low of $87,156, a 0.61% increase from the previous week's average price ($92,312). 3. Mining news worth noting includes: (1) Ethiopia seeks partners to explore Bitcoin mining layout; (2) Two independent miners produced blocks this week and received a reward of about $300,000; (3) NIP Group disclosed that it produced 151.4 BTC in the initial operation period of Bitcoin mining; (4) Bitdeer sold 146.8 BTC this week, and its Bitcoin holdings dropped to about 1,502; Data partner: Cango Inc. (CANG), a Bitcoin mining company listed on the New York Stock Exchange.

data.title: BitMart has listed DisclaimerCoin (DONT) in its "BM Discover" section.
data.description: BitMart's "BM Discover" section launched DisclaimerCoin (DONT) on January 23 at 12:00 (UTC+8), and the DONT/USDT trading pair was opened at this launch.

data.title: Fund managers' cash holdings hit a record low, reflecting extremely bullish investor sentiment.
data.description: Data from BofA's global fund manager survey shows that fund managers' cash holdings have fallen to a record low of 3.2%, reflecting investors' extremely bullish sentiment towards risky assets. (Barchart)

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Discusses Bitcoin's Origins and Principles
data.description: Vitalik Buterin recently shared his thoughts on Bitcoin's inception, emphasizing its foundational mindset. According to Foresight News, Buterin highlighted that Bitcoin was created with a resistance to the impulse of chasing universally popular tools. Instead, it adhered to principles that focus on building and utilizing tools that safeguard user autonomy and freedom.

data.title: Emerging Markets Embrace Stablecoins Amid Inflation Concerns
data.description:
Emerging markets increasingly adopt stablecoins, driven by inflation and cross-border remittance demand, according to S&P Global Ratings.
source: https://coincu.com/news/emerging-markets-stablecoins-adoption/

data.title: Whale Engages in High-Leverage Ethereum Trading
data.description:
On January 23, a whale executed a high-leverage trade involving 4,212 ETH at 11:16, according to BlockBeats. The position was opened with a 25x leverage at an average price of $2,962.62, currently showing a slight unrealized loss.

The address has been actively trading ETH and BTC recently, primarily engaging in short-term, high-leverage isolated positions.

data.title: MilkyWay Concludes Final Revenue Distribution Amid Shutdown
data.description: MilkyWay has completed its last protocol revenue distribution, allocating 92,708 USDC converted from fees in TIA, INIT, and BABY tokens to eligible community members. According to NS3.AI, participants in this distribution included MILK token holders, stakers, and liquidity providers, with a minimum threshold set at 1 USDC. The project is winding down and will permanently shut down due to lower-than-expected DeFi adoption and delayed funding solutions.

data.title: Nasdaq has applied to remove the 25,000-contract position limit for Bitcoin and Ethereum ETF options.
data.description: Nasdaq has filed a rule change application with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), seeking to remove the 25,000-unit position limit for spot Bitcoin and Ethereum ETF options, aligning crypto ETF options with rules for other commodity funds. Nasdaq stated that this move would give crypto ETF options equal treatment with other commodity options, eliminating unequal restrictions. The SEC has opened a comment period, with a final decision expected by the end of February. (Cointelegraph)
